title: New Sith
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the Sith faction founded by [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin]] in [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]]|[[Sith Empire (disambiguation)|another Sith faction]]|linebreak=1}}

}}
{{Quote|The so-called New Sith Wars lasted a thousand years. Many warlords during that time sought to control the destiny of the Sith Empire. They achieved victories from the Battle of Mizra to the Sictis Wars, but those were victories without a clear line of succession. Every Sith wanted to be king.|Darth Bane's writings|<ref name="BOS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>}}
The '''New Sith''', also known as the '''New Sith Empire''', the '''Sith Empire''' or simply as the '''Sith''', was a restored [[Politics/Legends|empire]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] that existed throughout the [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]]-long [[Draggulch Period]], which dissolved into several factions as time went on. The New Sith Empire arose in [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]] due to the machinations of [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who initiated the [[Fourth Great Schism]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], becoming a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. Amassing a multitude of like-minded disciples, Ruin and his empire proceeded to clash with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in the [[New Sith Wars]]. The reign of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Ruin ended prematurely due to the instigation of his backstabbing followers, setting a treacherous precedent that would plague his successors for many [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]]. Nonetheless, the New Sith had emerged into a [[Blood/Legends|bloody]] age of [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] expansion, eventually giving rise to a new leader—the shadowy "[[Dark Underlord]]" and his marauding [[Black Knights]]. Even with the Dark Underlord's [[Battle of Malrev IV|defeat]] by the Jedi, the Sith crusades persisted, further expanding their territory and power base.

[[1466 BBY|Over five hundred]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after their resurgence, the New Sith achieved a crushing victory against Republic and Jedi [[Army/Legends|forces]] at the [[Battle of Mizra]], provoking mass Jedi defections and a continued streak of Sith domination. In the wake of this upturn, the tyrannical [[Darth Rivan]] rose to leadership, creating a unique brand of [[warrior]]s—the [[Sith battlelord|Battlelords]]—in an attempt to curb the string of betrayals among the Sith. This venture met an [[Battle at Almas|abrupt end]], however, after Rivan and his Battlelords were entrapped, and ultimately destroyed, by a [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]] called the [[Darkstaff]]. In the [[1250 BBY|last centuries]] of the New Sith Wars, the reigning Dark Lord [[Belia Darzu]] created an [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Technobeast|mutated cyborgs]]—the [[Metanecrons]]—during the two-[[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] [[Sictis Wars]]. The end of this period saw yet another Sith victory, but not before Darzu's demise at the hands of the [[Mecrosa Order]].

By the [[1100 BBY/Legends|advent]] of the century-long [[Republic Dark Age]], the New Sith had all but completely eroded the strength of their ancient nemeses. Despite the Republic's imminent collapse, the Sith ranks devolved into a period of discord and treachery, plunging their empire into a [[Sith civil war|civil war]]. Throughout the Republic Dark Age, a number of Sith [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] took control of their own independent dominions, continuing their violent conquests against the Republic, the Jedi and each other. The fragmented New Sith remnants endured a phase of perpetual turmoil for some time—although still collectively dominating [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] Outer Rim—until the intervention of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|renegade Jedi]] [[Skere Kaan]] in [[1010 BBY]]. Declaring himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith, Kaan conquered the warring Sith enclaves, reorganizing them into a faction called the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. However, the Brotherhood met its end during the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]] in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], being deceived by [[Darth Bane/Legends|one of their own]] into destruction, allowing the Sith to finally die but paving way for the new [[Order of the Sith Lords]].

==History==
===Jedi schism===
{{Quote|A mysterious figure who may have been an Umbaran, Darth Ruin revived the Sith Order and seduced a number of Jedi to his cause. A war with the Jedi Order inevitably followed, but the Sith soon turned the war upon themselves. Ruin was destroyed by his own disciples, who ushered in a millennium-long period of betrayal and darkness amongst the Sith.|Bodo Baas's writings|<ref name="JvS" />}}
[[File:DarthRuin-EGF.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Darth Ruin initiated the Fourth Great Schism of the Jedi Order, founding a new Sith Empire.]]
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]], the beginning of the [[Draggulch Period]] brought about the revival of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]], a cult of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] practitioners who—over the past several [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]]—sought the destruction of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the peacekeeping [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="TEA" /> For [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] up to this point, the Sith were commonly believed to have been vanquished,<ref name="JvS" /> having been inactive since the reign of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] from the time of the [[Great Galactic War]]. The Sith eventually returned, however,<ref name="TEA" /> due to the machinations of a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] known as [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Phanius]]. Reputed for his solipsistic mindset, Phanius pursued alternative teachings in conflict with the Jedi Order's [[Jedi Code/Legends|prohibitions]],<ref name="END" /> leading him to uncover the ways of the Sith from within a rare [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]].<ref name="DSSB">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref>

Seduced by the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], the intelligent and charismatic Phanius abandoned the ways of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]],<ref name="CSWE-RUIN">{{CSWECite|III|114|Ruin, Darth}}</ref> intent on reviving the Sith Order. To this end, the fallen Jedi Master sought out and infiltrated a number of surviving [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith clans]], uniting them under his banner and assuming the identity of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Ruin.<ref name="END" /> In the process, fifty additional Jedi chose to leave the Order and rally behind Ruin's new Sith crusade, resulting in the [[Fourth Great Schism|Fourth]] [[Great Schism/Legends|Great Schism]].<ref name="TEA" /> Declaring himself a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Ruin drove his followers to wage war against the Jedi Order and the Galactic Republic,<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> founding a new [[Empire (disambiguation)|empire]] of the Sith and initiating a conflict known as the [[New Sith Wars]].<ref name="TEA" />

Throughout his reign over the New Sith, Darth Ruin garnered infamy as a particularly merciless and self-centered leader, known for sacrificing many of his underlings on a whim. This ultimately led to the Dark Lord's [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] at the hands of his own Sith disciples,<ref name="JvS" /> who were unwilling to tolerate their leader's egocentric philosophy.<ref name="END" /> Despite Ruin's [[Death/Legends|death]], his actions introduced a new age of expansion for the Sith, resulting in the reclamation of numerous Sith [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]—particularly [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] and [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]]—along with acts of sabotage against the Republic.<ref name="TEA" /> The treachery that led to Ruin's fall, however, also provoked a string of further betrayals among his Sith successors, which would persist for hundreds of years to come.<ref name="END" />

===Black Knights===
{{Quote|Young master, have you heard the tale of the Jedi Master Murrtaggh and the Dark Underlord?|A Bimm bard|<ref name="Heroes & Rogues">''[[Heroes & Rogues]]''</ref>}}
[[File:DarkUnderlordCropped.jpg|thumb|right|160px|The Dark Underlord consolidated a Sith alliance known as the Black Knights.]]
By [[1750 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> another individual had seized the Dark Lord mantle as the leader of the New Sith—a shadowy dark-side [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] known only as the "[[Dark Underlord]]."<ref name="END" /><ref name="DSSB" /> The origins of the Dark Underlord were shrouded in mystery; some speculated that he was summoned from the realm of [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]] itself, while others believed him to be a reincarnation of [[Xendor]],<ref name="END" /> a [[darksider]] who once lived during the [[First Great Schism]] thousands of years earlier.<ref name="TEA" /> A [[Blood/Legends|bloodthirsty]] marauder, the Dark Underlord proved himself a force to be reckoned with among the Sith, allowing him to consolidate a Sith alliance—the [[Black Knights]]<ref name="END" />—in a destructive campaign against the Republic and the Jedi.<ref name="CSWE-UNDERLORD" />

Headquartered on the planet [[Malrev IV]], the Black Knights were known for their violent exploits along the [[Zona Miki route]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorizing]] numerous [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] at the behest of the Dark Underlord. These exploits attracted the ire of [[Murrtaggh]],<ref name="TEA" /> a Jedi Master who hired a group of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] in a plot to sabotage the Dark Underlord's forces. The Mandalorians staged a [[Battle of Malrev IV|diversionary attack]] on Malrev IV, diverting the attention of a [[Unidentified Zeltron Sith general|leading Sith commander]] and consequently leaving the Dark Underlord vulnerable to Murrtaggh's own assault. In the ensuing battle, the Dark Underlord met his demise at the hands of the Jedi Master.<ref name="END" /> The Black Knights were not completely vanquished, however, and eventually managed to reform their ranks on several occasions—even centuries after the New Sith Wars—in the Dark Underlord's absence.<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>

===Battle of Mizra===
{{Quote|At the Battle of Mizra, the death of the Jedi coordinator to a Sith sniper turned the Jedi retreat into a full-scale slaughter.|Sabla-Mandibu's writings|<ref name="TJP" />}}
The death of the Dark Underlord ultimately did little to impede the momentum of the Sith war effort, which continued to grow over the following centuries. Despite a number of foiled incursions at [[Sith incursion at Corphelion|Corphelion]], [[Battle of Gap Nine|Gap Nine]] and [[Sith incursion at King's Galquek|King's Galquek]], the New Sith had enlisted allies among the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronians]] and [[Hiitian/Legends|Hiitians]] by the year [[1500 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> [[1466 BBY|Decades later]], the Sith amassed an [[Army/Legends|army]] against Republic and Jedi forces during the [[Battle of Mizra]]. Many [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lords of the Sith]] participated in the engagement, each mounting a personalized [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] named after a predatory [[Species/Legends|species]] that epitomized their innermost nature.<ref name="NEC" /> The tide of this engagement was turned by the actions of a [[Unidentified Sith sniper|lone Sith sniper]] who—after killing a [[Unidentified Jedi coordinator|Jedi coordinator]] and disrupting their [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]]—triggered a full-scale slaughter of the Jedi forces,<ref name="TJP" /> resulting in a crushing Sith victory.<ref name="NEC" /> At the battle's end, hundreds of Jedi were captured by the Sith and promptly converted to the ways of the dark side.<ref name="TEA" /> In the aftermath of their triumph at the Battle of Mizra, the Sith were left without a clear line of succession, leaving no immediate heir to the Dark Lord mantle.<ref name="BOS" /> Nonetheless, Mizra was a pivotal turning point in favor of the Sith, solidifying their power base and paving the way for a new leading Sith figure—the tyrannical [[Darth Rivan]].<ref name="TEA" />

===Tyranny of Rivan===
{{Quote|The light blinds us. It is only in the dark that we see clearly, and there is a great dark hidden among these worlds.|Darth Rivan|<ref name="The Darkstaff">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20030807darkstaff|text=The Darkstaff}}</ref>}}
[[File:Darthriv.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Darth Rivan used the power of Sith magic to overcome betrayal among the Sith ranks.]]
At some point between 1417 BBY and [[1250 BBY]],<ref name="EOTJ-Rivan">According to ''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'', [[Darth Rivan]] became a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] sometime between 1,150 and 1,400 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of the story. It can be deduced that ''Echoes of the Jedi'' takes place in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]; the story occurs two years after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which was declared in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. As such, the earliest time of Rivan's reign can be dated at 1417 BBY. Furthermore, ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' confirms [[1250 BBY]] as the beginning date for the rule of [[Belia Darzu]] (established as Rivan's successor in ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]''), thus providing the latest potential date for Rivan's reign.</ref> the Dark Lord Darth Rivan led the New Sith from a personal [[Almas Sith fortress|fortress]] on the [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] planet [[Almas]]. In an effort to overcome the perpetual betrayals among the ranks of the Sith, Rivan sought to build an army of darksiders led by a unique brand of Sith [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] known as the [[Sith battlelord|Battlelords]].<ref name="EOTJ" /> Through a series of complex [[Force power/Legends|rituals]] fueled by the power of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]],<ref name="SBs">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/lf20050901sithbattlelords|text=Sith Battlelords}}</ref> the Dark Lord created a [[Force-bond/Legends|Force bond]] between the Battlelords and the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] under their command, preventing the subordinates from attacking or killing their masters.<ref name="EOTJ" />

Initially, the use of Rivan's Battlelords brought a higher degree of cohesion to the armies of the Sith. This success proved short-lived, however,<ref name="AMA">{{LivingForce|A Mon Alone}}</ref> when the eventual betrayal of Rivan's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]<ref name="SBs" />—[[Darsin]]<ref name="EOTJ" />—obstructed the fruition of the Dark Lord's Battlelord army.<ref name="SBs" /> Additionally, the Jedi began specifically killing individual Battlelords, thereby releasing the bonded subordinates and allowing them to flee the battlefield. As a result, the Sith deemed the Battlelords unfit for their intended purpose, ultimately discontinuing their use.<ref name="AMA" />

During his reign, Darth Rivan obsessively sought the destruction of a [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]] known as the [[Darkstaff]],<ref name="EOTJ" /><ref name="Devan">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20030619devan|text=Devan}}</ref> fearing its reputably malevolent power as a potential threat to his existence.<ref name="EOTJ" /> In time, however, the Jedi Order staged an [[Battle at Almas|assault]] against Rivan's [[Fortress/Legends|stronghold]] on Almas,<ref name="JvS" /> precipitating a violent end to the Dark Lord's reign. By this point, Rivan had finally managed to acquire the Darkstaff, only to have its power turn against him amid the pivotal battle. To this end, the Darkstaff invoked a [[Wormhole/Legends|hyperspace wormhole]], obliterating Rivan's entire contingent of Battlelords and [[Time travel/Legends|teleporting]] the Dark Lord himself forward through [[Time/Legends|time]]. At his final destination on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] during the [[Light and Darkness War]], Darth Rivan—now [[Sever Force/Legends|deprived]] of his ability to wield the Force—was killed by a [[Unidentified Force-user (Darth Rivan)|Force-user]] of unremarkable power.<ref name="EOTJ" />

===Sictis Wars===
{{Quote|Maggot of metal, rust, and rot. Sith life draws breath, old life does not.|''Mechu-deru vitae'' incantation|<ref name="NEGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>}}
[[File:BeliaDarzuCropped.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Belia Darzu created the mutated technobeasts, the defining horror of the Sictis Wars.]]
In 1250 BBY, the final quarter of the Draggulch Period saw the rise of [[Belia Darzu]], a Dark Lord who led the New Sith during a two-[[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] period known as the [[Sictis Wars]].<ref name="TEA" /> Using a [[Belia Darzu's fortress|hidden stronghold]] on the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] world of [[Tython/Legends|Tython]],<ref name="ROT" /> Darzu experimented with the power of ''[[mechu-deru]]'', an obscure technique allowing the manipulation of mechanical structures through the Force.<ref name="NEGD" /> These experiments led to the creation of the [[nanogene spore]],<ref name="CSWE-NANO">{{CSWECite|II|359|nanogene spore}}</ref> a [[virus]] capable of transforming living victims into hulking [[Cyborg/Legends|hybrids of metal and flesh]] called [[technobeast]]s.<ref name="NEGD" /> Through the power of the dark side, the technobeasts were bound to the will of Darzu herself,<ref name="ROT" /> who promptly mobilized the creatures into an obedient army known as the [[Metanecrons]].<ref name="END" /> Over the course of the Sictis Wars, the technobeasts were unleashed upon the Galactic Republic and the Jedi,<ref name="TEA" /> becoming recognized as the defining horror of this period.<ref name="NEGD" />

By [[1230 BBY]],<ref name="MECROSA">Logically, the alliance between [[Belia Darzu]] and the [[Mecrosa Order]] must have fallen apart by [[1230 BBY]], the confirmed date of Darzu's death according to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''.</ref> an alliance once established between [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Darzu and the [[Mecrosa Order]]<ref name="ROT" />—a secret society from the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]]<ref name="END" />—had fallen apart.<ref name="ROT" /> The Mecrosa sought revenge against the Dark Lord for an unwanted incursion in their territory.<ref name="END" /> Additionally, Darzu—like many of her Sith predecessors—fell victim to treachery at the hands of her own Sith followers,<ref name="VADER">''[[Vader: The Ultimate Guide]]''</ref> culminating in the Dark Lord's [[Assassination of Belia Darzu|assassination]] by the Mecrosa.<ref name="ROT" /> In spite of Darzu's death and the resulting sabotage of her technobeast army,<ref name="END" /> the New Sith were deemed victorious by the end of the Sictis Wars.<ref name="BOS" /> Additionally, Darzu's successors continued utilizing the technobeasts for the remainder of the New Sith Wars, albeit in smaller numbers than before.<ref name="NEGD" /> The victory of the Sictis Wars came at a cost, however, and once again deprived the Sith of a clear line of succession for a new leader to seize the Dark Lord mantle.<ref name="BOS" />

===Fragmentation===
{{Quote|And so we argued, while the Republic grew frail—its citizens sickened with plague—and became isolated by a decaying infrastructure. Coruscant's throat lay bare beneath our blades, but the Sith plunged their knives into one another's backs instead!|Darth Bane's writings|<ref name="BOS" />}}
[[File:BrotherhoodOfDarkness-BoS.jpg|thumb|left|160px|The fragmented remnants of the New Sith were reformed into the Brotherhood of Darkness.]]
By [[1100 BBY/Legends|1100 BBY]], the relentless Sith crusades had weakened the Republic's economic, military and political strength to the point of absolute collapse, beginning an era known as the [[Republic Dark Age]].<ref name="TEA" /> This new era, however, also brought about a phase of intense division between the power-hungry Lords of the Sith. Unwilling to unite under a single leader, the New Sith failed to capitalize on the Republic's decline. Instead, they turned against one another in a [[Sith civil war|period of disagreement and backstabbing]], leaving their ranks irreparably fragmented.<ref name="BOS" /> The century-long Republic Dark Age saw the rise of many Sith [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] who<ref name="NEC" />—despite their lack of unity<ref name="BOS" />—continued waging their bloody conquests throughout entire [[Star system/Legends|star systems]],<ref name="NEC" /> with some maintaining control of their own independent territories.<ref name="KE">[[Knight Errant 0|''Knight Errant'' 0]]</ref> Even in their divided state, the Sith collectively dominated [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] Outer Rim while pushing towards the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Discord persisted between the fragmented remnants of the New Sith until the year [[1010 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> Around this time, [[Skere Kaan]]—a Jedi Master known for his radically disillusioned outlook on the Republic and the Jedi—had organized a group of like-minded peers called the "Brotherhood." Spearheading a campaign to conquer the remaining Sith enclaves on his own terms, Kaan defeated the most powerful of the warring Sith Lords in a matter of [[Standard month/Legends|months]], all while working under the false pretense of rebuilding the Republic. In the process, the Jedi Master<ref name="EGW" />—having since declared himself a Dark Lord of the Sith<ref name="END" />—assumed leadership over the surviving Sith, uniting them to the principle of "[[Rule by the Strong]]." Amassing his twenty-thousand-strong following, Kaan founded a new, unified Sith faction called the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]],<ref name="NEC" /> promptly declaring war against the Republic in the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] of the New Sith Wars.<ref name="EGW" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Darth Ruin, who left the Jedi Order to pursue his monomaniacal philosophy of self-interest and gave rise to the thousand-year war that concluded at Ruusan, and who was among the last to hold the Darth title—until I took it up again.|Darth Bane's writings|<ref name="BOS" />}}
[[File:DarthBane-frontal.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Darth Bane created the Rule of Two to overcome the pitfalls of his predecessors.]]
After the collapse of the New Sith,<ref name="TEA" /> Kaan and his Brotherhood of Darkness sought to repress the unrelenting betrayals that had ultimately caused the downfall of their predecessors.<ref name="BOS" /> To this end, Kaan allowed all members of the Brotherhood to bear the title of Dark Lord of the Sith, hoping to encourage a sense of equality throughout their ranks.<ref name="POD">''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''</ref> Additionally, Kaan forbade his followers from assuming the coveted title of Darth, believing that such a practice merely provoked strife among the Sith. Despite these preventative measures,<ref name="BOS" /> the Brotherhood met their ultimate demise in the climactic [[Ruusan campaign]], ending the thousand-year-long New Sith Wars in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> The Sith were not completely destroyed, however, as a surviving Brotherhood member—[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], who had orchestrated the Brotherhood's destruction through manipulations and deceptions, started a new [[Order of the Sith Lords]] and the [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]].<ref name="BOS" />

In the aftermath of the Draggulch Period,<ref name="TEA" /> succeeding generations of the Sith Order came to regard the New Sith Wars as the bloodiest, most wasteful conflict in galactic history.<ref name="END" /> The ramifications of the Fourth Great Schism were documented in a Jedi guidebook titled [[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force|''The Jedi Path'']], citing the Sith revival as the near-end of galactic civilization.<ref name="TJP" /> Nonetheless, the Republic avoided extinction and escaped the Dark Age perpetuated by the Sith, rebuilding its government after the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]].<ref name="TEA" /> In a collection of historical [[Book/Legends|writings]] called the ''[[Book of Sith]]'', Darth Bane lambasted the New Sith, asserting that their persistent infighting led them to waste an ideal opportunity to defeat the Republic.<ref name="BOS" /> This<ref name="END" />—combined with Bane's fervent opposition to the Brotherhood's promotion of equality<ref name="BOS" />–led him to establish the "[[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]]," a doctrine that was intended to curb the ambitions of the power-hungry Sith.<ref name="END" /> Under this doctrine, Bane's successors enacted a [[Grand Plan/Legends|centuries-long plan]] for galactic domination, eventually succeeding where the New Sith had failed by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|overthrowing the Republic]] and all but completely [[Order 66/Legends|purging]] the Jedi Order.<ref name="BOS" />

By the year [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the New Sith and a number of their leading figureheads were discussed in a [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force|historical compendium]] about the Force, compiled by [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]].<ref name="JvS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The New Sith were first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], authored by [[Terry Brooks]] and published in [[1999]]. The novel establishes the New Sith (simply dubbed the "Sith" at the time) as the first Sith Order to exist in spite of having been founded "almost two thousand years ago,"<ref name="TPM">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]]</ref> contradicting the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] comics.<ref name="TOTJ">[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' comic series]]</ref> In the year [[2000]], this contradiction was reconciled in [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], a [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|reference book]]—penned by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Daniel Wallace]]—which first identified the faction as the New Sith.<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> The ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' misattributed the history of the New Sith under the name of the Brotherhood of Darkness,<ref name="POTJ">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> a separate organization that existed after the New Sith collapsed.<ref name="TEC" /> While most commonly known as the New Sith in multiple sources, the faction was later referred to as the New Sith Empire in the [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.27|sixth volume]] of [[Star Wars Comic|''Star Wars Comic UK'']], released in [[2011]].<ref name="CWUK">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.27|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' Comic 6.27]]</ref> In the [[2012|following year]], the group was simply named the Sith Empire in ''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''.<ref name="BOS" />
